popularStory.headline: Ramon Berenguer IV (d 1162) Bio Sketch
popularStory.description:
BIO:  Marquis of Barcelona, 1131; Count of Barcelona; King of Aragon, 1150-62.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/ARAGON%20&%20CATALONIA.htm#Petronilladied1174B, as of 11/3/2014

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona於1114年出生在法國米迪-列內斯艾維倫羅德茲時，他的父親Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona34歲，母親Douce de Gévaudan28歲。他於1151年與Rainha Petronilla de Aragón在西班牙阿拉格昂斯卡斯卡結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1162年8月6日在義大利皮蒙特辛博爾戈 三 達爾馬左享年48歲，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納利波爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona于1114年出生在法国米迪-列內斯艾維倫羅德茲时，他的父亲Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona34岁，母亲Douce de Gévaudan28岁。他于1151年与Rainha Petronilla de Aragón在西班牙阿拉格昂斯卡斯卡结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1162年8月6日在意大利皮蒙特辛博爾戈 三 達爾馬左享年48岁，埋葬在西班牙卡塔倫亞赫羅納利波爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Barcelona
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Amelita, Elvira, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Mariano, Orlando, Ramon, Romelia.

Catalan and Spanish: habitational name from Barcelona, the principal city of Catalonia. The placename is of uncertain, certainly pre-Roman, origin. The settlement was established by the Carthaginians, and according to tradition it was named for the Carthaginian ruling house of Barca; the Latin form was Barcino or Barcilo.

variants.name: Barcelo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Carlos, Mario, Francisco, Juan, Ruben, Arturo, Eduardo, Humberto, Ignacio, Jaime.
Catalan (Barceló): apparently from the Latin personal name Barcelonus, a male equivalent of Barcelona, originally denoting someone from the city of Barcelona (see Barcelona ).

nameMeanings.name: Ramon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Berenguer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish and French (southern; Bérenguer): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements ber ‘bear’ + ger ‘spear’, a cognate of French Bérenger (see Berenger ) and Béringer (see Beringer ).

variants.name: Berenger
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Berringer  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona was born in 1114, in Rodez, Aveyron, Midi-Pyrénées, France, his father, Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona, was 34 and his mother, Douce de Gévaudan, was 28. He married Rainha Petronilla de Aragón in 1151, in Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Spain. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 6 August 1162, in Borgo San Dalmazzo, Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy, at the age of 48, and was buried in Ripoll, Girona, Catalonia, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona est né en 1114, à Rodez, Aveyron, Midi-Pyrénées, France, son père, Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Douce de Gévaudan, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Rainha Petronilla de Aragón en 1151, à Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 août 1162, à Borgo San Dalmazzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italie, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Ripoll, Girona, Catalunya, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona 1114 in Rodez, Aveyron, Midi-Pyrénées, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona 34 und seine Mutter Douce de Gévaudan 28. Er hat 1151 in Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Spanien Rainha Petronilla de Aragón geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. August 1162 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Borgo San Dalmazzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italien und wurde in Ripoll, Girona, Catalunya, Spanien begraben.

Lui sposato Rainha Petronilla de Aragón nel 1151, nel Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelonaが1114年にフランスミディ-プイルeンeスエイビーロンロデズで生まれた時，父Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelonaは34歳，母Douce de Gévaudanは28歳であった。1151年にスペインアラグoンハエスカハエスカでRainha Petronilla de Aragónと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1162年8月6日にイタリアピエモントクウネオボルゴ サン ダルマズゾで48歳で死去し，スペインカタルニーアギロナリポルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona(이)가 1114년 프랑스 미디-p이ee스 에비런 로데즈에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Douce de Gévaudan(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1151년 스페인 아라그o 후스카 후스카에서 Rainha Petronilla de Aragón(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1162년 8월 6일 이탈리아 피몽 쿤오 볼고 산 다마즈오에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카털니아 지로나 리폴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona nasceu em 1114, em Rodez, Aveyron, Midi-Pyrénées, França, seu pai, Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Douce de Gévaudan, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Rainha Petronilla de Aragón em 1151, em Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 6 de agosto de 1162, em Borgo San Dalmazzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Itália, com 48 anos, e foi sepultado em Ripoll, Girona, Catalunya, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona родился в 1114 г. в местности родез, авирон, миди-пирeнeесь, Франция, его отцу по имени Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Douce de Gévaudan, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Rainha Petronilla de Aragón в 1151 г. в местности хуеска, хуеска, арагoн, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 6 августа 1162 г. в местности борго сан далмацзо, кунео, пиемонт, Италия в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности риполл, гирона, каталуня, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ramon Berenguer IV de Barcelona nació en 1114, en Rodez, Aveyron, Mediodía-Pirineos, Francia, su papá, Raymond Bérenger III de Barcelona, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Douce de Gévaudan, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Rainha Petronilla de Aragón en 1151, en Huesca, Huesca, Aragón, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 6 de agosto de 1162, en Borgo San Dalmazzo, Cuneo, Piemonte, Italia, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultado en Ripoll, Gerona, Cataluña, España.
